subject: [PATCH v6 01/22] mm/swap: decouple swap cache from physical swap infrastructure

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed questioned the design decision to decouple swap cache from physical swap infrastructure, suggesting that reusing the swap table as the representation of virtual swap space could be a more efficient approach.
reviews.raw_body:
I didn't look through the entire series, but let me ask the same
high-level question I asked before. Instead of moving things out of the
swap table, why not reuse the swap table as the representation of the
virtual swap space? Seems like most/all metadata is already moved there
in a nice concise format.

2026-05-11.patch_summary: This patch decouples the Linux kernel's swap cache from its physical swap infrastructure, preparing for virtualization of swap space. The swap cache is currently managed at the physical swap layer, but will be moved to a virtual swap layer in future patches. To achieve this, the patch removes swap cache related helpers from the swap table and introduces a single global lock to synchronize swap cache accesses. This change also sets the stage for re-partitioning of the swap cache into virtual swap clusters.
